domains.name: Executable MBSE
domains.blurb: Model-Based Systems Engineering models that compute, not just describe: SysML v2 with analysis and constraint definitions, and executable Capella.

domains.name: MathWorks Model-Based Design
domains.blurb: Simulink, Simscape, and Stateflow reference applications and whole-system examples. Access is typically campus- or employer-licensed.

domains.name: Multidisciplinary Design Optimization
domains.blurb: Frameworks that couple disciplines and optimize whole vehicles or systems, such as OpenMDAO and the aircraft tools built on it.

domains.name: Equation-Based Physical Modeling
domains.blurb: Acausal, equation-based modeling of multi-domain physical systems: Modelica and its commercial peers, plus the FMI co-simulation standard.

domains.name: Controls and Dynamics in Python
domains.blurb: Open Python libraries for control system design, optimization, and model-predictive control.

domains.name: Discrete-Event and Agent-Based Simulation
domains.blurb: Tools that simulate systems of many interacting entities or agents over time, from factories to traffic networks to social systems.

domains.name: Power and Energy Systems
domains.blurb: Power flow, distribution, capacity expansion, and building energy models for grids and energy systems.

domains.name: Infrastructure Networks
domains.blurb: Hydraulic and hydrologic models of water distribution and stormwater networks.

domains.name: Robotics and Multibody
domains.blurb: Robot descriptions and physics engines that simulate articulated, multi-body mechanical systems.

domains.name: Aerospace and Space Mission
domains.blurb: Flight dynamics, spacecraft attitude and orbit, mission analysis, and aircraft geometry tools.

domains.name: Wind and Marine Energy
domains.blurb: Aeroelastic, wake, and wave-energy-converter models for renewable energy devices and farms.

domains.name: Process and Chemical Engineering
domains.blurb: Steady-state and dynamic process simulators and benchmark plants for chemical engineering.

domains.name: Circuits and Electronics
domains.blurb: SPICE-class circuit simulators for analog and mixed-signal electronics.

domains.name: Architecture Description Languages with Analysis
domains.blurb: Architecture description languages whose models drive analyses such as schedulability, safety, and resource budgets.

domains.name: Parametric Spreadsheet Models
domains.blurb: Openly shared spreadsheet engineering models where inputs drive recomputed outputs, such as link, mass, and power budgets.

domains.name: Computing Design Structure Matrix Models
domains.blurb: Design Structure Matrix models that compute change propagation or clustering, as distinct from static matrices.

domains.name: Heterogeneous and Digital-Twin Platforms
domains.blurb: Platforms that compose multiple models of computation or couple simulators into co-simulations and digital twins.

domains.name: Systems Biology (adjacent)
domains.blurb: Curated, runnable SBML models of biological systems, included only where framable as a runnable multi-element engineered-style system.

domains.name: Continuum Solvers (adjacent)
domains.blurb: Continuum mechanics solvers such as computational fluid dynamics, included only where framable as a runnable multi-element system.

entries.name: OpenMDAO
entries.gloss: An open-source framework for gradient-based multidisciplinary design optimization of coupled engineering systems.
entries.system_represented: Coupled multidisciplinary engineering systems — for example an aircraft whose aerodynamics, structures, and propulsion are optimized together — assembled from interconnected analysis components.
inputs_you_can_change: design variables (e.g. geometry, sizing, operating conditions)
outputs_you_can_observe: objective and constraint values at the optimum
entries.runtime_required: Python 3 (with NumPy/SciPy; optional pyOptSparse for advanced optimizers)

scale.element_count: Framework, not a fixed model — representative coupled models assemble dozens to hundreds of components, variables, and connections.
scale.grouping: Components are nested into Groups (subsystems), which compose into the overall model.
scale.notes: An "element" here is a Component or a connection in the model dependency graph.

entries.suitability: Suits engineers and researchers comfortable with Python who need rigorous, derivative-based optimization of coupled systems; steeper learning curve than a point tool, so less suited to a casual newcomer.
entries.executability_justification: A user defines design variables, an objective, and constraints, runs the optimizer, and observes a changed optimum and changed coupled-analysis outputs — varying inputs produces observably different outcomes.

source_per_field.maintained_by: NASA Glenn Research Center (MDAO Lab), per openmdao.org

verification_confidence.notes: Version and license confirmed against PyPI on the verification date.
